digest.component_or_area: release workflow + Hatchling provenance build hook
propositions: The release artifact and the CI-tested code come from different build paths, and the built wheel/sdist is published without any functional install test before publication.

The provenance hook writes _build_info.py unconditionally and bakes COMMIT = None when built from an extracted sdist that lacks a .git tree, so the published wheel carries a null commit while the sdist keeps the real SHA.
The only test asserting a non-null baked SHA builds WheelBuilder directly against the checkout, bypassing the failing sdist-to-wheel path so the defect is uncaught.

Restructure the release to build wheel+sdist once, upload one named bundle with SHA256SUMS, test the exact artifacts in clean isolated jobs, and publish the same bytes after hash verification with no rebuild.
Fix the hook to read REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT (short SHA) as primary source with git fallback, failing only when the env var is set but unresolvable so dev pip installs degrade gracefully.
Bake and assert a short SHA to stay consistent with signing.py's short-SHA gate_code_version output, and prove env-var propagation through python -m build into the wheel-from-sdist path via a test.
